data.narrative:
Black Forest Labs is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Robin Rombach, Andreas Blattmann, and Patrick Esser, having raised $200M+ and currently valued above $1B. The peer universe comprises 9 private and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with 5 direct same-sector, same-stage peers ranging from $500M to $32.0B in valuation.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $200M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yielding an implied valuation of $380M. Peer-median analysis across five same-stage AI & Software companies—Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M)—produces a median of $5.0B, reflecting substantial dispersion in the peer set.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $323M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.7B. The stage-progression result sits notably below peers, suggesting either undervaluation relative to comparables or that Black Forest Labs' disclosed capital raise underweights equity value relative to peer financing rounds. The peer median is driven upward by outliers like Safe Superintelligence; the interquartile range ($1.0B–$5.6B) better captures central tendency. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given rapid valuation inflation in frontier AI, and whether the current valuation reflects recent funding rounds or market repricing.
